codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the masonry gallery component. The MasonryGallery.tsx (336 lines) covers all 8 assigned validation assertions: full-screen masonry layout, empty state with instructional text, image tiles with filename/label overlay and download, video tiles with play overlay and inline playback, responsive multi-column layout via ResizeObserver, independent scrolling with overscroll containment, loading indicator during generation, and Download All wired to JSZip via the existing downloadAllArtifacts hook. The component is properly integrated into both CreativeSparkDesktopLayout and CreativeSparkMobileLayout, replacing placeholder content. Code follows existing patterns (base64 data URLs, framer-motion animations, ActivitySpinner reuse, existing utils for formatFileSize/getArtifactSize). Lint and build pass cleanly.

issues.description: JSDoc comment says 'shortest-column-first approach' but the actual implementation at line 35-38 is simple round-robin (index % columnCount). The comment is misleading. Round-robin is a reasonable approximation when tile heights are unknown pre-render, but the docstring should match reality.

Dead ternary branch in cn() call for VideoTile overlay className. Both branches of `isPlaying ? '...' : '...'` produce identical class strings ('opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 group-active:opacity-100'). The cn() could be simplified to a single static string.

Uses standalone `import type { BrainstormArtifact }` instead of the inline type modifier `import { type BrainstormArtifact }` recommended by the frontend-worker skill and frontend-knowledge.md. Not a functional issue — linter accepts both forms.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The masonry implementation uses round-robin column distribution rather than true shortest-column-first masonry packing. For images of varying aspect ratios, this can produce uneven columns. The project doesn't document a preferred masonry algorithm, so the worker made a reasonable choice — but if true height-aware masonry is expected, this should be documented as a known limitation or convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: MasonryGallery.tsx:30-38 — distributeItems() uses `index % columnCount` round-robin, not height-based allocation

sharedStateObservations.observation: Hardcoded MIME types in data URLs (image/png, video/mp4) are used across the entire codebase rather than reading from artifact.mimeType. This is consistent with the existing pattern in ArtifactRow.tsx and ArtifactPreview.tsx, and the backend only produces these two types. However, if new image formats (webp, jpeg) are ever introduced, this would break silently. Could be worth documenting this assumption.

summary: Pass. The masonry gallery implementation is complete and covers all 8 assigned validation assertions (VAL-GALLERY-001 through -005, -011, -012, -014). Created MasonryGallery.tsx with EmptyState, ImageTile, VideoTile sub-components, responsive column layout via ResizeObserver, framer-motion animations, ActivitySpinner for loading, and Download All button. Properly integrated into both desktop and mobile Creative Spark layouts. Three non-blocking issues found: misleading JSDoc comment about distribution algorithm, dead ternary branch in cn() call, and standalone import type usage instead of inline style. Lint and build pass. No blocking issues.
